title: Thrichittatt Maha Vishnu Temple
tagline: Thrichittatt Maha Vishnu Temple — spiritual town of Kerala

overview.short: The Thrichittatt Mahavishnu Temple (also called Thiruchenkundrur and Imayavarappan temple) is a Hindu temple dedicated to Vishnu, located in Chengannur, Alappuzha District, Kerala,
overview.description: The Thrichittatt Mahavishnu Temple (also called Thiruchenkundrur and Imayavarappan temple) is a Hindu temple dedicated to Vishnu, located in Chengannur, Alappuzha District, Kerala, South India. Constructed in the Kerala style of architecture, the temple is glorified in the Nalayira Divya Prabandham, by Nammalvar, one of the Sri Vaishnava saint-poets of the 8th century called the Alvars. It is one of the 108 Divya Desams dedicated to Krishna, an avatar of Vishnu, who is worshipped as Imayavarappan. howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Old Syrian Church, Chengannur
topPlaces.description: Pazhaya Suriyani Pally a.k.a. Old Syrian Church, Chengannur is an ancient Syrian Christian church of Kerala.

topPlaces.name: Mundankavu
topPlaces.description: Mundankavu is a village situated 2 kilometres (1.2 mi) from Chengannur town centre in Alappuzha district, Kerala, India. It lies on the banks of the river Pampa.

topPlaces.name: Chengannur Mahadeva Temple
topPlaces.description: Chengannur Mahadeva Temple is a prominent Hindu temple, dedicated to Shiva and located in the town of Chengannur in the South Indian state of Kerala. The temple is one of the major Shiva temples in Kerala, along with the Ettumanoor Mahadevar Temple, Kaduthruthy Mahadeva Temple, Vaikom Temple, Ernakulam Shiva Temple and Vadakkunnathan Temple.

topPlaces.name: Kadavil Malika
topPlaces.description: Kadavil Malika is a historic place in India, associated with the origin of the Maramon Convention. It is a house in Kallissery on the banks of River Pamba and was built by Unnittan Kathanar (1767–1852) and his son Abraham Kathanar (1822–1884) also known as Kadavil Achen, in the early 19th century.

topPlaces.name: Kallisseri Azhakiyakavu Devi Temple, Chengannur
topPlaces.description: Azhakiyakavu Devi Temple is situated at Kallisseri in Chengannur taluk of Alappuzha district, Kerala, India. It is a Hindu temple dedicated to the goddess Bhadrakali and is one of the 30 temples in Central Travancore area where the ancient Dravidian ritualistic art form of Padayani is performed annually.

topPlaces.name: Othera
topPlaces.description: Othara or Othera is an Indian village in Kerala state. It is located in Taluk Tiruvalla, Revenue Division Thiruvalla in Pathanamthitta district.

topPlaces.name: Perissery
topPlaces.description: Perissery is a village situated 1 kilometre (0.62 mi) west of Chengannur Railway Station in Alappuzha district, Kerala, India.

topPlaces.name: Sree Narayanapuram Thrikkayil Temple
topPlaces.description: Sree Narayanapuram Thrikkayil Temple (Malayalam: ശ്രീ നാരായണപുരം തൃക്കയിൽ ക്ഷേത്രം) is one of the oldest temples in Perissery. The divine idol installed here represents the world–enchanting form of Krishna endowed with the four lustrous arms carrying the conch, the discus, the mace, and the lotus.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Thrichittatt Maha Vishnu Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Thrichittatt Maha Vishnu Temple is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Thrichittatt Maha Vishnu Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Cochin International Airport, Kochi (94 km) and the nearest railway station is Ernakulam Junction (Kochi) (80 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Thrichittatt Maha Vishnu Temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Thrichittatt Maha Vishnu Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Thrichittatt Maha Vishnu Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Thrichittatt Maha Vishnu Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Thrichittatt Maha Vishnu Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Old Syrian Church, Chengannur, Mundankavu, Chengannur Mahadeva Temple, Kadavil Malika, Kallisseri Azhakiyakavu Devi Temple, Chengannur.
